summary: First backend-only pass on IntelligenceOS. 34/39 tests passing (87%). All AI agents (contradiction, management, materiality, earnings_diff, bias, market_brief, portfolio_brief, portfolio/connections, portfolio/macro, thesis assumption_check, journal/analyze) returned real GPT-5.2 JSON output – Emergent LLM integration is healthy. yfinance flows (market/overview, quote, history, company profile/financials/news, search, watchlist, portfolio KPIs, screener, DCF, graph, timeline) all work. Found 3 CRITICAL server-side bugs: (1) route-ordering shadow makes GET /api/thesis/living return [] always, (2) POST /api/alerts returns 500 due to ObjectId serialization, (3) POST /api/journal returns 500 due to same ObjectId bug (blocks the whole Decision Journal flow – list/postmortem/analyze).

critical.issue: Route is shadowed by the earlier-declared GET /api/thesis/{ticker}. Any GET on /api/thesis/living (with or without ?ticker=…) is matched by the legacy get_thesis(ticker='living') handler and returns [] from the wrong collection (db.theses filtered by ticker='LIVING'). GET /api/thesis/living/{id}, /diff, /history, POST /api/thesis/living, and the /check endpoint are unaffected (different path segments/methods).
critical.repro: 1) POST /api/thesis/living with a valid payload → 200. 2) GET /api/thesis/living/<id> → returns the doc. 3) GET /api/thesis/living → returns []. 4) GET /api/thesis/anything_else → also returns [] with the same shape (confirms shadow).
critical.fix: In /app/backend/server.py, move the @api.get('/thesis/living') declaration (line ~826) BEFORE @api.get('/thesis/{ticker}') (line ~496); or delete the legacy /thesis/{ticker} endpoint if unused; or rename it to /thesis/legacy/{ticker}.

critical.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error. `db.alert_rules.insert_one(r)` mutates the dict in place by adding `_id: ObjectId(...)`. The endpoint then returns that same `r`, and FastAPI's jsonable_encoder fails with `TypeError: 'ObjectId' object is not iterable`.
critical.fix: Return `{k: v for k, v in r.items() if k != '_id'}` — the same pattern used in create_living_thesis / add_pipeline.

critical.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error – identical ObjectId serialization bug on `entry` dict after `db.journal_entries.insert_one(entry)`. This blocks the entire Decision Journal feature (list/analyze/postmortem all depend on entries being created).
critical.fix: Return `{k: v for k, v in entry.items() if k != '_id'}` before returning.

minor.issue: Same ObjectId-in-response bug as /api/alerts and /api/journal. Not exercised by current app pages but will 500 if called.
Cookie set with samesite='none' and secure=True; only usable over HTTPS. Not tested end-to-end here (no live Google session); JWT bearer fallback in get_current_user works.
yfinance intermittently returns price=None when rate-limited. Endpoint still 200s; cache TTL is only 60s. Consider longer TTL / stale-while-revalidate for reliability.

action_items: Fix route-ordering: move @api.get('/thesis/living') above @api.get('/thesis/{ticker}') in server.py (or delete legacy). This is currently why the Living Thesis LIST page will always look empty.

Strip Mongo _id before returning inserted docs in create_alert (/api/alerts) and create_journal (/api/journal). Suggested pattern: `return {k: v for k, v in doc.items() if k != '_id'}`. Apply the same fix to legacy create_thesis for consistency.
After fixes, re-run pytest -q /app/backend/tests to confirm 39/39.
critical_code_review_comments: Route ordering: FastAPI matches paths in registration order. /thesis/{ticker} declared before /thesis/living silently swallows the list route. Prefer more-specific routes first, or use unambiguous prefixes.

Motor's insert_one mutates the input dict with _id. Three endpoints (create_thesis legacy, create_alert, create_journal) return the mutated dict → 500 on serialization. Adopt a single helper like `def _strip_id(d): return {k:v for k,v in d.items() if k != '_id'}` and use everywhere.
server.py is 1113 lines and contains auth, market, portfolio, thesis, journal, pipeline, agents, valuation, RAG, graph — consider splitting into routers per domain.

AGENT_SYSTEM string prompts are duplicated logic; strict-JSON instruction is appended in run_agent. Fine, but no schema validation on the LLM output beyond .setdefault — malformed JSON silently returns fallback with 'Analysis unavailable'.
portfolio/macro parses LLM 'factor:score' lines by regex-ish digit extraction. If model returns 'interest_rates: 65-75', it silently truncates. Consider structured tool-use / json schema.
yfinance calls are synchronous inside async endpoints — will block the event loop under load. Wrap in run_in_executor for production.

success_rate.backend: 87% (34/39 tests passing – 2 failed, 3 blocked-by-fixture; root cause = 3 server bugs above)

test_credentials: Test users are created dynamically via POST /api/auth/signup with TEST_iops_<rand>@example.com / TestPass!234. See /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest session creates a fresh user, 3 portfolio holdings (AAPL/MSFT/NVDA), a watchlist add (AMD), a Living Thesis v1+v2 for AAPL, and 2 journal entries. Data prefixed with TEST_ where applicable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the 3 issues (route order + 2 ObjectId returns), re-run `cd /app/backend && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<preview> python -m pytest tests/ -v` — expect 39 passed. All AI endpoints already verified working with GPT-5.2 via Emergent LLM key. yfinance works but is rate-limit-sensitive; treat null prices as warnings not failures.
rca_of_the_issue: 1) Living-thesis LIST bug: FastAPI resolves routes in declaration order. The legacy get_thesis handler on /thesis/{ticker} (line 496) is declared BEFORE list_living_theses on /thesis/living (line 826). GET /thesis/living therefore matches /thesis/{ticker} with ticker='living', querying db.theses (not db.living_theses) filtered by ticker='LIVING' → always []. Fix: reorder declarations or namespace the routes. 2 & 3) Motor's insert_one mutates the input document adding a BSON ObjectId under `_id`. create_alert and create_journal return that mutated dict, which FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize (ObjectId is not JSON-serializable and not iterable/dict-able) → 500. Fix: exclude `_id` from the response like create_living_thesis / add_pipeline already do.
